endpoint_label: general anti-inflammatory activity summary
claim_text_raw: Updated review summarizing anti-inflammatory mechanisms and activities of tea bioactive compounds across studies.

endpoint_label: general gut microbiota modulation summary
claim_text_raw: Updated review summarizing gut microbiota-related functional activities of tea bioactive compounds.

endpoint_label: extraction technologies affecting bioactive compound recovery
claim_text_raw: Review describing how extraction technologies influence recovery of value-added tea bioactive compounds.

endpoint_label: processing-associated phytochemical profile change
claim_text_raw: Review linking black tea harvesting and processing steps to phytochemical profile and final quality.

endpoint_label: gut microbiota composition and function modulation
claim_text_raw: Review on differently processed tea and gut microbiota effects across tea types.

endpoint_label: bioavailability as determinant of in vivo activity interpretation
claim_text_raw: Review arguing that tea polyphenol bioavailability is essential for interpreting mechanisms of action and health effects in vivo.

endpoint_label: cardiovascular disease mortality risk
dose_exposure: habitual tea consumption as reported in included cohorts
claim_text_raw: Meta-analysis of prospective cohorts indicating an association between tea consumption and reduced cardiovascular disease mortality risk.

endpoint_label: all-cause mortality risk
dose_exposure: habitual tea consumption as reported in included cohorts
claim_text_raw: Meta-analysis of prospective cohorts indicating an association between tea consumption and reduced all-cause mortality risk.

endpoint_label: cognition and mood related outcomes in healthy participants
dose_exposure: dose and duration as reported in included RCTs
host_phenotype: Systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs on tea or L-theanine or L-theanine plus caffeine reporting cognition-related outcomes in healthy participants.

endpoint_label: body weight gain liver/adipose accumulation and liver fat reduction in HFD mice
mechanism_label: gut barrier preservation; TLR4 signaling inhibition; inflammatory cytokine suppression
claim_text_raw: Tea polyphenols attenuated HFD-induced weight gain, liver and adipose accumulation, improved liver fat content, and suppressed inflammatory signaling while modulating gut microbiota and SCFAs.
notes: Refined from PubMed abstract/snippet with taxa and mechanism details

endpoint_label: gut microbiota and SCFA modulation with barrier protection in HFD mice
mechanism_label: G protein-coupled receptor activation; histone deacetylase inhibition; tight junction enhancement; barrier protection
host_phenotype: intestinal barrier integrity and reduced inflammation state
claim_text_raw: Tea polyphenols increased Blautia and Faecalibaculum, reduced Colidextribacter, activated G protein-coupled receptors, inhibited histone deacetylases, enhanced intestinal tight junction expression, and preserved intestinal barrier integrity.
notes: Refined from PubMed abstract/snippet with taxa and barrier-mechanism details

endpoint_label: cognitive decline microglia activation and synaptic plasticity impairment in circadian rhythm disorder model
model_system: rodent cognitive impairment model induced by circadian rhythm disorder
mechanism_label: microglia activation reduction; intestinal barrier protection; BDNF-PSD95-SYN synaptic plasticity support
claim_text_raw: OTP relieved microglia activation, reduced LPS and glutamate-related neurotoxicity, increased SCFAs, upregulated Muribaculaceae and Clostridia_UCG-014, downregulated Desulfovibrio, and improved synaptic plasticity markers including BDNF, PSD-95, and SYN.
notes: Refined from PubMed abstract with named taxa/metabolites and synaptic markers

endpoint_label: obesity-related outcomes via gut microbiota review summary
claim_text_raw: Review summarizing fermented tea effects on gut microbiota and obesity-related outcomes.

endpoint_label: gut microbiota modulation in individuals with and without obesity with stronger effect in obesity
model_system: human participants with and without obesity
dose_exposure: regular black tea kombucha consumption for 8 weeks as reported
claim_text_raw: Regular black tea kombucha consumption positively influenced gut microbiota in both normal and obese groups, with enrichment of commensal bacteria such as Bacteroidota and Akkermansiaceae and reduction of Ruminococcus and Dorea, especially in the obese group.
notes: Human microbiome intervention refined from PubMed abstract and review snippet

endpoint_label: fasting glucose reduction and circulating endotoxin reduction in adults with gut-barrier improvement
model_system: healthy adults and adults with metabolic syndrome
dose_exposure: decaffeinated catechin-rich green tea extract confection as reported
mechanism_label: reduced small intestinal permeability; reduced gut inflammation
claim_text_raw: Catechin-rich green tea extract decreased circulating endotoxin and fasting glucose, likely by reducing gut inflammation and small intestinal permeability.
notes: Human barrier/glucose mechanism refined from PubMed abstract

endpoint_label: systemic inflammatory marker outcomes in adults
model_system: healthy adults and adults with metabolic syndrome
dose_exposure: decaffeinated catechin-rich green tea extract confection as reported
mechanism_label: reduced gut inflammation without systemic cytokine change
claim_text_raw: The trial suggested reduced gut inflammation and improved barrier function but without improvement in systemic inflammation markers.
notes: Negative-control style human inflammation record refined from PubMed abstract

endpoint_label: airway inflammation suppression in asthmatic mice and airway epithelial cells
model_system: asthmatic mice and human airway epithelial cells
confidence_score: Green tea extract suppressed airway inflammation via oxidative stress-driven MAPKs/MMP-9 signaling.

endpoint_label: oxidative stress reduction in asthmatic model
model_system: asthmatic mice and human airway epithelial cells
confidence_score: Green tea extract reduced oxidative-stress-related signaling in the asthmatic model.

endpoint_label: body composition and obesity-related hormone outcomes
confidence_score: Dose-response meta-analysis of randomized trials on green tea extract supplementation and body composition/obesity-related hormone outcomes.

endpoint_label: oxidative stress marker outcomes in adults
confidence_score: Green tea extract supplementation improved oxidative stress markers in randomized trials.

endpoint_label: oxidative stress and antioxidant status markers in adults
confidence_score: Green tea improved oxidative status markers including TAC, SOD, GPX and reduced MDA in the meta-analysis.

endpoint_label: inflammatory marker outcomes in adults
confidence_score: Green tea showed limited or mixed effects on inflammatory markers, with improvement not consistent across CRP, IL-6 and TNF-alpha.

endpoint_label: body weight reduction improved insulin sensitivity and adipose browning in diet-induced obesity
claim_text_raw: BMTP significantly reduced body weight, improved insulin sensitivity, decreased adipocyte volume, increased multivesicular adipocyte-like cells, and upregulated UCP1 in iWAT and iBAT.
notes: Refined from PubMed abstract and ACS snippet

endpoint_label: butyrate production and gut microbiota-driven adipose thermogenesis
mechanism_label: butyrate-mediated adipose browning; PGC-1alpha/UCP1 thermogenic programming
claim_text_raw: BMTP increased serum and colonic butyric acid levels and shifted gut microbiota composition; antibiotic treatment worsened obesity while butyrate supplementation partially restored body weight insulin sensitivity and thermogenic proteins PGC-1alpha and UCP1.
notes: Refined from PubMed abstract and ACS snippet

endpoint_label: body weight fat accumulation liver steatosis and dyslipidemia improvement in HFD mice
mechanism_label: reduced pro-inflammatory cytokines; metabolic syndrome attenuation
microbiota_taxon: taxa to be verified from full text
claim_text_raw: TFPS significantly mitigated HFD-induced metabolic syndrome, including reductions in body weight, fat accumulation, liver steatosis, TC, TG, LDL-C, and pro-inflammatory cytokines TNF-alpha, IL-6, and IL-1beta, with increased IL-10.
notes: Phenotype and cytokine details refined from PubMed abstract

endpoint_label: gut microbiota modulation linked to metabolic syndrome attenuation
microbiota_taxon: taxa to be verified from full text
claim_text_raw: Tea flower polysaccharide attenuated metabolic syndrome in association with modulation of gut microbiota in HFD-induced mice.
notes: Kept conservative on taxa pending full-text check

endpoint_label: hepatic cholesterol accumulation and NAFLD progression reduction in mice
mechanism_label: alternative bile acid synthesis activation; FXR-FGF15-FGFR4-ASBT suppression; FXR-SHP pathway modulation
microbiota_taxon: Muribaculaceae; Pseudomonas; acterium_coprostanoligenes_group; Clostridiales; Lachnospiraceae_NK4A136_group; Delftia; Dubosiella; Romboutsia
microbial_metabolite: conjugated bile acids; non-12OH bile acids
claim_text_raw: YTP altered colonic bile-acid composition, increased conjugated and non-12OH bile acids, decreased FXR, FGF15, FGFR4, and ASBT, increased Cyp7a1 and Cyp27a1, and inhibited hepatic cholesterol accumulation in NAFLD mice.
notes: Added bile-acid pathway and taxa from PubMed abstract

endpoint_label: gut microbiota and bile acid metabolism modulation in mice with BSH-related remodeling
mechanism_label: bile salt hydrolase-related microbial remodeling; taurine-metabolism genera increase
microbiota_taxon: Muribaculaceae; Pseudomonas; acterium_coprostanoligenes_group; Clostridiales; Lachnospiraceae_NK4A136_group; Delftia; Dubosiella; Romboutsia
microbial_metabolite: conjugated bile acids; non-12OH bile acids
claim_text_raw: YTP reshaped the intestinal microbiome by decreasing BSH-producing genera and increasing taurine-metabolism genera, with multiple genera strongly correlated with specific bile-acid monomers.
notes: Added BSH/taurine-metabolism mechanism and taxa list from PubMed abstract

endpoint_label: oxidative stress reduction and antioxidant enzyme restoration in aging mice
mechanism_label: NF-kB suppression; antioxidant enzyme restoration; oxidative stress reduction
claim_text_raw: TFPS improved gut microbiota homeostasis, reduced the Firmicutes to Bacteroidota ratio, increased Lactobacillus, increased SOD CAT and POD activity, and reduced MDA in aging mice.
notes: Added gut-microbiota and oxidative-stress marker details from PubMed abstract

endpoint_label: microglial activation and neuroinflammation reduction in aging mice
claim_text_raw: TFPS reduced excessive hippocampal microglial activation, down-regulated IL-6 IL-1beta TNF-alpha and NF-kB, and alleviated neuroinflammation in aging mice while regulating gut microbiota.
notes: Added neuroinflammation and microbiota detail from PubMed abstract

endpoint_label: gut microbiota alteration in mice
claim_text_raw: Tea polysaccharides altered gut microbiota and physiological health indicators in mice.

endpoint_label: physiological health indicator improvement in mice
claim_text_raw: Tea polysaccharides improved physiological health indicators in mice alongside gut microbiota changes.

endpoint_label: obesity amelioration with enhanced adipocyte thermogenesis in high-fat diet mice
claim_text_raw: Heimao tea polysaccharides ameliorated obesity by enhancing gut microbiota-dependent adipocyte thermogenesis in mice fed a high-fat diet, with Dubosiella and Romboutsia closely associated with the anti-obesity effect.
notes: Added named taxa from PubMed abstract

endpoint_label: gut microbiota-dependent adipocyte thermogenesis mechanism in obese mice
claim_text_raw: Dubosiella and Romboutsia increased significantly and were negatively correlated with body weight and positively correlated with iBAT index in HMTP-treated obese mice.
notes: Added taxa-correlation information from PubMed abstract
